title: Captain-managed private notes on each player ('no-nos' live here)
description: The place for things players shouldn't have to express about themselves — 'plays better apart from X', 'always late on Tuesdays', scheduling quirks. Notes are visible to any admin-session user (captain/admin), never to the player, never rendered on any token-accessed page. Edited in place from the dashboard and from the admin player detail page.
acceptance_criteria: From a dashboard player row, an admin can add / edit / delete a captain_player_note for that player
Notes have two kinds: Partnership (e.g. 'works better apart from X') and General (scheduling, comms)
Notes are authored — author_user_id and updated_at are recorded and displayed alongside the note body
Notes appear on the admin player detail page (Sprint 016 WI-099) in a clearly labelled 'Captain notes (private)' section
Notes NEVER appear on /my-profile/*, /my-availability/*, or any page reachable via a fantasy token
Handler code explicitly asserts admin session auth and contains a comment forbidding any token-route inclusion
Deleted notes are hard-deleted (simpler; document the choice) — no audit trail for v1
Notes visible on the planning matrix row as a small icon — click opens the note list in a popover

leak_prevention: Convention: captain_note_repository is never imported by any package under internal/players/*
E2E regression test in WI-107 asserts absence on token-accessed pages

testing_requirements: Integration test: note creation records author and timestamp
E2E: WI-107 covers the leak regression

context.user_story: As a captain, I need a place to record discreet things — partnerships that don't click, someone's scheduling quirk — without putting that responsibility on the player to self-disclose.
context.design_principle: Negative framing is captain-owned. The player only ever sees and edits positive-framed fields about themselves.
